description: Grounding map: every quantitative claim is traced to its data source.
paper_version: v9 (B2 math rigor, 210-config calibration, 6-method baseline, kappa4 sensitivity, Z3 convergence)

claims.claim: O(1/N)+O(1/N^2) corrections validated across 358 configurations: 4 activations x 3 depths x 8 sigma_w x 5 widths. 1.1-6.4x improvement in ordered/critical regime.
claims.verification: 80 Monte Carlo trials per config. Covers relu, tanh, gelu, silu; depths 5,10,20; widths 32-2048.

claims.claim: O(1/N)+O(1/N^2) corrections reduce variance error: 3.7%->1.1% at W=32, 3.0%->1.5% at W=64, 1.0%->0.7% at W=128
claims.verification: 80 Monte Carlo trials per width, depth=5, sigma_w=1.35, ReLU

claims.claim: Phase classification with >=20 seeds per sigma_w across 4 activations. ReLU: 0 dangerous errors. tanh/gelu: 0 dangerous errors. SiLU: 1 boundary-adjacent dangerous error at sw=1.943 (sw*=1.993). All non-SiLU errors are boundary type.
claims.verification: 20 seeds per sigma_w value, failure taxonomy (conservative/boundary/dangerous), adaptive sampling.

claims.claim: Three-class phase classification achieves 83.3% at D=10 W=512 (up from 56% baseline)
claims.verification: 18 sigma_w values x 5 seeds per point. Superseded by v4 with 20 seeds.

claims.claim: Perturbative expansion converges when L*|chi_1-1|*D/N << 1. Diverges in deep chaotic regime (chi_1 >> 1) as expected.
claims.verification: Correction ratio < 0.5 enforced by clamping. Soundness Theorem 4 in paper.

claims.claim: ResNet variance recursion: q^{l+1} = q^l + 2a*sw^2*C(q^l) + a^2*(sw^2*V(q^l) + sb^2) where C(q) = E[z*phi(z)]. ResNet chi_1 > 1 for all alpha > 0.
claims.verification: 11 unit tests covering cross-covariance, chi_1, phase comparison, depth improvement, small alpha convergence.

claims.claim: Calibration diagnostics: ECE, MCE, ACE, Brier score decomposition, reliability diagrams for soft phase posteriors.
claims.verification: 8 unit tests covering perfect/random calibration, ECE bounds, multiclass report, Brier decomposition.

claims.claim: Soundness conjecture (empirically supported): mean-field convergence, finite-width correction order, phase classification well-definedness, convergence radius condition.
claims.verification: 3 unit tests for convergence, correction scaling, and chi_1 continuity. Assumptions A1-A4 explicitly listed. Relabeled from Theorem to Conjecture.

claims.claim: ReLU closed-form verification: kappa=0.5, sw*=sqrt(2), chi_2=0, E[relu^4]=3q^2/8, E[relu^6]=15q^3/48, E[phi_prime^2]=1/2.
claims.verification: 8 unit tests, all exact to 6+ decimal places.

claims.claim: ReLU chi_2=0 (degenerate bifurcation, relu double-prime = 0 a.e.)
claims.verification: Analytical: ReLU is piecewise linear, second derivative is zero

claims.claim: tanh chi_2=0.0385 (supercritical bifurcation)
claims.verification: Numerical integration E[tanh_double_prime(z)^2] via scipy.integrate.quad

claims.claim: GELU chi_2=9.622 (supercritical bifurcation)
SiLU chi_2=3.902 (supercritical bifurcation)
Critical/He init outperforms Xavier by 1.4-2.1x on regression tasks
claims.verification: 5 seeds x 2 widths x 2 depths, 300 SGD steps

claims.claim: ReLU sw*=1.414 with CI narrowing from [1.17, 1.63] at W=128 to [1.35, 1.47] at W=2048
claims.verification: Bootstrap over O(1/sqrt(N)) chi_1 fluctuations, 100 samples per width

claims.claim: tanh sw*=1.010 with CI narrowing from [0.83, 1.93] at W=128 to [0.96, 1.31] at W=2048
ReLU edge-of-chaos: sw* = sqrt(2) ~ 1.414
claims.verification: Closed-form: chi_1 = sw^2/2, set to 1 gives sw = sqrt(2)

claims.claim: tanh edge-of-chaos: sw* = 1.010
claims.verification: Brent method + scipy.integrate.quad, tolerance 1e-8

claims.claim: GELU edge-of-chaos: sw* = 1.982
claims.verification: Brent method + scipy.integrate.quad, tolerance 1e-8

claims.claim: SiLU edge-of-chaos: sw* = 1.993
claims.verification: Brent method + scipy.integrate.quad, tolerance 1e-8

claims.claim: ReLU kurtosis excess kappa = 0.5
claims.verification: Analytical: E[ReLU^4] = 3q^2/8, E[ReLU^2] = q/2, kappa = 3/2 - 1 = 0.5

claims.claim: Binary trainability prediction achieves 100% accuracy
claims.verification: 27 runs: 5-layer ReLU MLPs at 9 sw values, 3 seeds

claims.claim: 55 unit tests passing, covering ResNet MF, calibration, expanded MF, ReLU closed-form, perturbative convergence, soundness, chi_2 bifurcation.
claims.verification: pytest run: 55 passed in 773.67s

claims.claim: Third-order susceptibility chi_3 computed for all activations: ReLU chi_3=0.0000 (degenerate), tanh chi_3=0.0039, GELU chi_3<0.001, SiLU chi_3<0.001. Confirms chi_2 suffices for normal form classification.
claims.verification: Numerical integration via scipy.integrate.quad at edge-of-chaos q*. For smooth activations, chi_3 << chi_2, confirming supercritical classification.

claims.claim: Formal O(1/N^3) truncation error bounds: |R_3| <= sigma_w^8 * M_8(q) / N^3. ReLU@W=64: |R_3|<=5.1e-2, tanh@W=64: |R_3|<=3.7e-4.
claims.verification: Bound computed from eighth moment E[phi^8] via analytical (ReLU) or numerical (others) integration.

claims.claim: Conv2d mean-field recursion identical to MLP (Xiao et al. 2018). Finite-width corrections scale as O(1/C_out). Phase diagram generation for CNN architectures.
claims.verification: 5-layer Conv(64,3x3,ReLU) at sigma_w=sqrt(2) gives chi_1=1.000 (critical). Phase diagram matches MLP predictions.

claims.claim: ResNet variance recursion q^{l+1} = q^l + 2α·σ_w²·C(q^l) + α²(σ_w²·V(q^l) + σ_b²) is consistent between abstract, Section 1 contribution (v), Section 7, and implementation.
claims.verification: All four locations use the same formula after fix. Cross-checked with Yang & Schoenholz 2017.

claims.claim: Z3 SMT proofs cover ReLU algebraic identities (P1-P7) only. Soundness conjecture is not mechanically verified. P5 is a tautology noted for completeness.
claims.verification: Paper now explicitly states scope: 'Z3 proofs cover ReLU-specific algebraic identities, not the full soundness conjecture.'

claims.claim: Critical window epsilon(N,phi) = c_phi * ln(D)/D with activation-specific scaling (c_relu=1.0, c_tanh=1.2, c_gelu=c_silu=1.8). Justification: chi_1^L requires |chi_1 - 1| ~ ln(L)/L for bounded gradient accumulation.
claims.verification: Activation-specific constants calibrated against empirical phase boundaries in v4 experiments.

claims.claim: LeakyReLU, Mish, and ELU supported with full moment computation. Edge-of-chaos: LeakyReLU sw*=1.4141, Mish sw*=1.6610, ELU sw*=1.0067.
claims.verification: All new activations validated: chi_1=1.0 at sw* within tolerance 0.01.

claims.claim: PyTorch integration: analyze() and recommend_init() functions accept torch.nn.Module and return phase classification with initialization recommendations.
claims.verification: Tested on MLP, CNN architectures. Automatic activation detection, sigma_w extraction, and ArchitectureSpec generation.

claims.claim: Independent calibration: 210 configurations (7 activations x 5 depths), 54.3% three-class accuracy (CI [47.5%, 60.9%]), 80.5% binary accuracy (CI [74.6%, 85.3%]). Ordered precision 93.8%, chaotic precision 82.7%. Dangerous error rate 1.4% (3/210).
claims.verification: 210 configs: 7 activations x 5 depths x 6 sigma_w values, width 256, 20 trials per config. Variance-ratio ground truth.

claims.claim: Soundness conjecture with Z3-verified convergence components. Contraction mapping for ReLU (sigma_w^2 < 2) and LeakyReLU verified. Truncation bound and perturbative validity propositions added.
claims.verification: Conjecture in paper with explicit Z3 theorem references. Truncation bound (Proposition) with full proof. Perturbative validity (Proposition) backed by kappa4 sensitivity data.

claims.claim: Bayesian phase classifier renamed to soft phase classifier throughout code and paper. The classifier uses heuristic window widths, not actual Bayesian inference.
claims.verification: All references to 'Bayesian' replaced except in bibliography (Neal 1996).

claims.claim: Head-to-head comparison: PhaseKit vs Xavier vs Kaiming vs LSUV vs MetaInit vs GradInit across 31 valid configs. PhaseKit wins 7/31 (22.6%), LSUV wins 10/31 (32.3%). PhaseKit excels on non-ReLU activations at depth >= 10.
claims.verification: 6 methods, 31 valid configs (4 depths x 2 widths x 4 activations), 2 seeds per method per config, 300 SGD steps.

claims.claim: Case study: 10-layer GELU MLP. Xavier/Kaiming fail (ordered phase, chi_1=0.25). PhaseKit diagnoses and recommends sigma_w=1.416, achieving 1.41x improvement.
claims.verification: Full diagnostic workflow: architecture → PhaseKit analysis → diagnosis → recommended sigma_w → training improvement.

claims.claim: chi_2 values corrected for consistency between Tables 3 and 4. Previous Table 3 double-counted sigma_w^2 factor. Correct values: GELU chi_2=2.454, SiLU chi_2=0.984, tanh chi_2=0.0382.
claims.verification: Values computed as sigma_w^2 * E[phi''(sqrt(q*) z)^2] and verified against numerical integration.

claims.claim: 210-configuration calibration: 54.3% three-class, 80.5% binary accuracy. Per-class: ordered precision 93.8% recall 45.0%, critical precision 23.6% recall 74.3%, chaotic precision 82.7% recall 57.3%. Dangerous errors: 3/210 = 1.4%.
claims.verification: All numbers cross-validated against JSON. Wilson 95% CIs computed.

claims.claim: Moment-closure sensitivity: 100% phase stability (50/50 configs) under ±50% kappa4 perturbation. Max boundary shift 0.44% (ReLU at width 256). 5 activations x 2 widths x 5 perturbation factors.
claims.verification: All 50 configs retain phase identity. Max shift validated against JSON.

claims.claim: Z3 SMT verification of convergence properties: 5 theorems, 14 sub-properties, all pass. ReLU contraction (sigma_w^2 < 2), chaotic expansion, LeakyReLU contraction, exponential convergence rate, depth-scale-phase correspondence.
claims.verification: 5/5 theorems, 14/14 sub-properties verified unsat.

claims.claim: Six-method baseline comparison (31 configs): PhaseKit 22.6%, LSUV 32.3%, MetaInit 19.4%, Kaiming 16.1%, Xavier 9.7%, GradInit 0%.
claims.verification: Win counts validated against JSON.

claims.claim: Truncation bound: |R_3| <= sigma_w^8 * M_8(q) / N^3. Full proof via Marcinkiewicz-Zygmund inequality in Appendix B.
claims.verification: Proof complete in paper. ReLU and tanh numerical values verified.

claims.claim: Perturbative validity under moment-closure perturbation: 100% phase stability under ±50% kappa4, max boundary shift 0.44%.
claims.verification: Proposition in paper backed by kappa4 sensitivity experiment data.

claims.claim: Transformer mean-field variance propagation achieves 2.9% mean relative error across 12 MiniGPT configurations (4-12 layers, d_model 64-256).
claims.verification: 12 configs, per-block variance ratios compared against empirical Monte Carlo.

claims.claim: Transformer phase classification achieves 98.9% accuracy (178/180 configs) with 100% critical-phase precision. Critical: 52/54 (96.3%), Chaotic: 126/126 (100%).
claims.verification: 180 configurations, 6 architectures x 30 sigma_w values, variance-ratio ground truth.

claims.claim: PhaseKit outperforms LSUV 4-0 on GPT-2-style transformer models (4L-12L), achieving lower mean final cross-entropy loss on all tested configurations.
claims.verification: 4 configs, 50 Adam steps each, PhaseKit mean loss 6.242 vs LSUV 6.253.

claims.claim: Compositional MF engine achieves 83% phase agreement across 18 architectures spanning 6 families (MLP, CNN, ResNet, DenseNet, UNet, Transformer).
claims.verification: 18 architectures, phase classification compared against empirical variance-ratio ground truth from forward hooks.

claims.claim: Block-aware chi computation correctly classifies Transformers as critical and ResNets as near-critical, fixing the prior bug where all normalized architectures were misclassified as ordered.
claims.verification: compute_chi_total() resets chi product at normalization boundaries; residual-aware path accounts for skip connections maintaining chi >= 1.

claims.claim: 8 Z3-verified properties (P13-P20) covering finite-width correction sign, magnitude bound, perturbative validity, monotonicity, ReLU/LeakyReLU kappa_4 closed forms, truncation bound non-negativity, and ReLU indicator idempotence.
claims.verification: All 8 properties verified (unsat) by Z3 SMT solver using QF_NRA logic.

claims.claim: Dataset-aware phase analysis tested on 240 configurations (3 activations x 2 depths x 2 widths x 5 sigma_w x 4 tasks) showing spectral alignment variation across tasks.
claims.verification: 240 configs, kernel-task spectral alignment (kappa) computed, trainability accuracy compared with and without data-aware correction.
